2606.24391.title: Age of LLM: A Strategic 1v1 Benchmark for Reasoning, Diplomacy and Reliability of Large Language Models under Fog of War
2606.24391.paper_summary: We introduce Age of LLM, a turn-based 1v1 benchmark in which two LLMs face off on a 13x7 grid to destroy the enemy base. Three stressors are deliberate: fog of war, full diplomacy (messages, ceasefires, ultimatums; uranium kept secret), and a reliability dimension where every turn must follow a strict JSON schema and an illegal action is silently discarded. The engine is private and each match uses a fresh random map seed and opponent, mitigating the data contamination that affects public benchmarks. Models receive a (near) rule-only prompt with no build-order advice (two tactical seed phrases were present during data collection; see Section 2.7). We benchmark 15 reasoning models across 54 matches and 5,258 actions. Findings: (1) the nuclear rush dominates (78% on the rules-coherent v0.11+ sub-corpus; 85% corpus-wide) with a sole-launcher signature that is largely mechanical under secret-simultaneous launch rules, not a cognitive deterrence failure; (2) military conquest is rare but faster (12.3 vs 18.9 turns); (3) diplomacy is prolific yet almost never consummated; (4) ~58% of illegal actions are fog/state errors, making the illegal-action rate a measure of belief-tracking; (5) -- the least established, and the only one we label exploratory -- a weak link associates reliability with winning. The corpus is small, unbalanced and not side-swapped, so the ranking is a preliminary descriptive view, not a contribution. Beyond ranking, the turn-by-turn traces of actions and messages make the corpus a lens on how LLMs reason under adversarial uncertainty -- their belief-tracking, spontaneous deception, and per-model cognitive "personas" -- which we frame as a future research direction. We release the replay format, an isometric viewer and all replays; engine source on request.
2606.24391.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹《LLM的時代》，這是一個回合制的1對1基準測試，其中兩個LLM在一個13x7的網格上對抗，目的是摧毀敵方基地。三個壓力因素是故意設置的：戰爭迷霧、全面外交（信息、停火、最後通牒；鈾保持秘密）以及一個可靠性維度，其中每個回合必須遵循嚴格的JSON架構，非法行動會被靜默丟棄。引擎是私有的，每場比賽使用新的隨機地圖種子和對手，減輕了影響公共基準的數據污染。模型接收一個（近乎）僅包含規則的提示，沒有建造順序建議（在數據收集期間存在兩個戰術種子短語；見第2.7節）。我們在54場比賽和5,258個行動中對15個推理模型進行基準測試。研究結果：（1）核武衝擊主導（在規則一致的v0.11+子語料庫中佔78%；在整體語料庫中佔85%），其單發射器特徵在秘密同時發射規則下主要是機械性的，而不是認知威懾失敗；（2）軍事征服雖然罕見但更快（12.3回合對18.9回合）；（3）外交繁榮但幾乎從未實現；（4）約58%的非法行動是由於迷霧/狀態錯誤，這使得非法行動率成為信念追蹤的一種衡量；（5）-- 最不確定的，也是我們標記為探索性的 -- 一個弱連結將可靠性與勝利相關聯。語料庫規模小、不平衡且未進行側置，因此排名只是初步的描述性觀點，而不是貢獻。除了排名之外，逐回合的行動和信息痕跡使得語料庫成為一個透鏡，觀察LLM在對抗性不確定性下的推理——他們的信念追蹤、自發欺騙和每個模型的認知“人格”——我們將其框定為未來的研究方向。我們發布了重播格式、一個等距視圖器和所有重播；引擎源代碼可按要求提供。
2606.24391.author: Arnaud Ricci et.al.
